methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series B → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.9x) to $400M last post money.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 5 same-sector + same-stage peers in Climate & Energy. Range: $120M - $5.0B.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Climate & Bio-Manufacturing

data.narrative:
Sage Geosystems, a Series B climate and energy company founded by Cindy Taff and Lance Cook, is valued at $400M post-money following $114M+ in cumulative fundraising. The valuation analysis draws from a peer set of 5 same-sector, same-stage private companies—Commonwealth Fusion Systems ($5.0B), Pano AI ($500M), Reverion ($310M), CarbonCapture Inc. ($300M), and Cemvita Factory ($120M)—alongside one public comparable (Lonza Group, ticker LNZA).

Two triangulation methods are applicable. The stage-progression approach applies a 1.9x multiplier to the $400M last post-money valuation, implying $760M. The peer-median method benchmarks against the five same-stage, same-sector comps, yielding a median valuation of $310M with a range from $120M to $5.0B. The wide dispersion reflects heterogeneous business models and technology risk profiles within the climate tech sector. Blending these two methods produces a triangulated valuation range: $264M (low), $535M (median), and $874M (high).

The $535M median sits above Sage's current $400M valuation, suggesting modest upside under comparable multiples. Key uncertainties include the wide peer range, the outsized influence of Commonwealth Fusion Systems as an outlier, and stage-to-stage step-up variability in frontier tech. Further diligence on Sage's relative technology risk and capital efficiency versus peers would inform placement within the derived range.
